pi_items.source: setup_task.sh creates diagram at this path (line 45)
setup_task.sh creates data file at this path (line 10)
Verifier checks if final_count > initial_count + 20; initial diagram has ~10-15 shapes, so 35 is appropriate target. export_result.sh analyzes mxCell count.
Task description (task.json lines 21-24) specifies three phases: 'Python 2 Era (2.0-2.7)', 'Transition Period (2.6-2.7 and 3.0-3.3)', 'Modern Python 3 (3.4-3.13)'
Data file includes Python 3.0 | Dec 3, 2008. Verified via Python.org official release page and multiple sources confirming December 3, 2008 release date.
Data file includes Python 3.6 | Dec 23, 2016 with f-strings feature. Verified via Python.org official documentation (What's New In Python 3.6) and PEP 498 confirming December 23, 2016 release.
Data file includes Python 3.11 | Oct 24, 2022. Verified via multiple Python version tracking sources confirming October 24, 2022 release.
Data file includes Python 3.13 | Oct 7, 2024. Verified via Python.org official release pages confirming October 7, 2024 release.
Data file: Python 3.4 | Mar 16, 2014 | asyncio, pathlib, enum, pip bundled. Verified via Python 3.4 documentation confirming asyncio was first released with Python 3.4.
Data file: Python 3.6 | Dec 23, 2016 | f-strings, variable annotations, secrets. Verified via Python.org What's New In Python 3.6 and PEP 498 confirming f-strings feature in Python 3.6.
Data file: Python 3.8 | Oct 14, 2019 | Walrus operator :=, positional-only params. Verified via Python.org What's New In Python 3.8 and multiple sources confirming walrus operator (:=) in Python 3.8.
Data file: Python 3.10 | Oct 4, 2021 | Structural pattern matching (match/case). Verified via Python.org What's New In Python 3.10 confirming structural pattern matching (PEP 634) in Python 3.10.

Data file includes: Python 2.7 | Jul 3, 2010 | OrderedDict, set literals, EOL: Jan 1 2020. Verified via PEP 373, Python.org sunsetting page, and multiple sources confirming Python 2.7 EOL was January 1, 2020.

privileged_info_summary: All metadata claims verified. The task involves creating a Python release timeline diagram in draw.io from a data file containing accurate historical Python release information. All required Python versions (3.0, 3.6, 3.11, 3.13) exist with correct release dates verified against official Python documentation. All required keywords (asyncio, f-strings, walrus operator, pattern matching) are present in the data file associated with their correct Python versions (3.4, 3.6, 3.8, 3.10 respectively). The Python 2.7 EOL date of January 1, 2020 is correctly documented. File paths for diagram, data, and export destinations are correctly configured. Expected minimum shape count of 35 aligns with task requirements for adding 13 Python 3.x versions plus containers and annotations. The data is synthetic (created during task setup) but contains accurate information verified against authoritative sources (Python.org, PEPs, release documentation).
